data.body: ROOT CAUSE (was UNCONFIRMED, now confirmed): an ordering bug. add_dependency emitted the promotion REDIRECT record to stdout BEFORE the durable LINK commit; when '| head' closed the pipe, the print raised BrokenPipeError and aborted before committing — the link was lost while the pipeline exit (head's, no pipefail) showed 0. relates_to is never promoted and prints nothing, so it always persisted — exactly the observed asymmetry. FIX (branch worktree-civil-marlin-flare, commit 9abce29f): compose the redirect up front but EMIT it only AFTER the LINK commit (durable data first). A closed pipe now raises only post-commit (persisted + loud), satisfying write-or-fail-loudly. Pending PR/merge before close.
